fact: May 31 is exactly 151 days into the year - that's the 151st Pokémon, Mew!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when baby raccoons typically start getting brave enough to leave their dens and waddle around like tiny bandits.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most places north of the equator on May 31, making it one of the earliest sunrise days of the year.
May 31 marks the end of meteorological spring - scientists officially call June 1 the start of summer, even though it doesn't feel like it yet!
Dandelions are usually going completely bonkers right now, shooting their fluffy seed parachutes everywhere like nature's confetti cannons.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, May 31 is deep in autumn when leaves are crunching underfoot and animals are getting chunky for winter.
The zodiac switches from Taurus to Gemini around May 21, so by May 31, we're firmly in Gemini territory - the chatty twin sign.
Fireflies typically start blinking their butts like crazy around this time in North America, turning backyards into magical light shows.
May 31 sits exactly one month before the summer solstice, when days are getting noticeably longer but haven't peaked yet.
In northern countries like Canada, this is often when ice finally melts off lakes, making that awesome crackling and groaning sound as it breaks apart.
Strawberries are usually getting plump and red in gardens right now, which is why June is called strawberry season - but May 31 is like the preview day!
The moon phase on May 31 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, you get some of the darkest, starriest skies of late spring.
